Package org.key_project.util.java

Class CollectionUtil

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.util.java.CollectionUtil
public class CollectionUtil extends Object
Provides static methods to work with Collections.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    SEPARATOR
    The default separator.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    addAll(Collection<T> collection, Iterable<T> iterable)
    Adds all elements to the Collection.

    binaryInsert(List<T> list, T toInsert, Comparator<T> comparator)
    Performs a binary insert on the given sorted List.

    Checks if the given element is contained in the given Iterable.

    Checks if the given two Collections contains the same elements in any order.

    Counts the number of elements in the given Iterable which are selected by the given IFilter.

    Converts the Collection into a String.

  • Field Details

    • SEPARATOR

      public static final String SEPARATOR
      The default separator.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • indexOf

      public static <T> int indexOf(Iterator<T> iter, T toSearch)
      Returns the index of the element to search in the given iterator.
      Parameters:
      iter - The iterator to search in.
      toSearch - The element to search.
      Returns:
      The index of the element or -1 if it was not found.

    • toString

      public static String toString(Collection<?> collection)
      Converts the Collection into a String.
      Parameters:
      collection - The Collection to convert.
      Returns:
      The Collection as String.

    • toString

      public static String toString(Collection<?> collection, String separator)
      Converts the Collection into a String and uses the defined separator to separate elements.
      Parameters:
      collection - The Collection to convert.
      separator - The separator between elements.
      Returns:
      The Collection as String.

    • isEmpty

      public static boolean isEmpty(Collection<?> collection)
      Nullpointersave execution of Collection.isEmpty().
      Parameters:
      collection - The given Collection.
      Returns:
      true = is empty or null, false = is not empty.

    • isEmpty

      public static boolean isEmpty(Map<?,?> map)
      Nullpointersave execution of Map.isEmpty().
      Parameters:
      map - The given Map.
      Returns:
      true = is empty or null, false = is not empty.

    • addAll

      public static <T> void addAll(Collection<T> collection, Iterable<T> iterable)
      Adds all elements to the Collection.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of the Collections elements.
      Parameters:
      collection - The Collection to add to.
      iterable - The elements to add.

    • removeComplete

      public static <T> boolean removeComplete(Collection<T> collection, T toRemove)
      Removes all occurrences of the element in the given Collection.
      Parameters:
      collection - The Collection to remove from.
      toRemove - The element to remove.
      Returns:
      true if at least one element was removed, false if the Collection was not modified.

    • searchAll

      public static <T> List<T> searchAll(Iterable<T> iterable, Predicate<T> filter)
      Searches all elements accepted by the given IFilter.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The Iterable to search in.
      filter - The IFilter to use.
      Returns:
      The elements accepted by the given Predicate.

    • search

      public static <T> T search(Iterable<T> iterable, Predicate<T> filter)
      Searches an element in the given Iterable instance.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The instance to search in.
      filter - The filter to select an element.
      Returns:
      The found element or null if no element was found.

    • searchAndRemove

      public static <T> T searchAndRemove(Iterable<T> iterable, Predicate<T> filter)
      Searches an element in the given Iterable instance and removes the found element from it.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The instance to search in.
      filter - The filter to select an element.
      Returns:
      The found element or null if no element was found.

    • searchAndRemoveWithException

      public static <T, E extends Throwable> T searchAndRemoveWithException(Iterable<T> iterable, IFilterWithException<T,E> filter) throws E
      Searches an element in the given Iterable instance and removes the found element from it.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The instance to search in.
      filter - The filter to select an element.
      Returns:
      The found element or null if no element was found.
      Throws:
      E extends Throwable

    • contains

      public static <T> boolean contains(Iterable<T> iterable, T element)
      Checks if the given element is contained in the given Iterable.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The given Iterable to search in.
      element - The element to search.
      Returns:
      true = contained, false = not contained

    • count

      public static <T> int count(Iterable<T> iterable, Predicate<T> filter)
      Counts the number of elements in the given Iterable which are selected by the given IFilter.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The elements to count in.
      filter - The IFilter to select elements.
      Returns:
      The number of elements selected by the IFilter in the given Iterable.

    • containsSame

      public static <T> boolean containsSame(Collection<T> first, Collection<T> second)

      Checks if the given two Collections contains the same elements in any order.

      Empty Collections and null parameters are treated as equal.

      Parameters:
      first - The first Collection.
      second - The second Collection.
      Returns:
      true both Collections contains same elements, false Collections are different.

    • removeFirst

      public static <T> T removeFirst(Iterable<T> iterable)
      Removes the first element from the given Iterable.
      Parameters:
      iterable - The Iterable to remove first element from.
      Returns:
      The removed first element or null if no element was removed.

    • binaryInsert

      public static <T> void binaryInsert(List<T> list, T toInsert, Comparator<T> comparator)
      Performs a binary insert on the given sorted List.
      Parameters:
      list - The sorted List to insert in.
      toInsert - The element to insert.
      comparator - The Comparator to use.
